name: The Antimonopolist
subject: Law and Oligarchy (and Farming)
body:
Dear friends, I've been teaching a class called The Law of Democracy for over a decade now, using a casebook that is taught around the country. As the whole structure of government teeters, and people have been disgusted with corruption for decades, it can feel a little strange, even grandiose, to call the laws governing American political processes "the law of democracy." Whatever it is that we have now, there is a temptation to act, act, and act more, or at least engage in the simulacrum of | | | | [](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/0a86b2ee?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)
---
[The Antimonopolist](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/d7ba4c32?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)
| [Law and Oligarchy (and Farming)](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/b1560b54?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)
---
| By Zephyr Teachout • 2 Oct 2025 | [View in browser](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/ae6889cf?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)
---|---
[View in browser](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/b8ecb5df?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)
Farmers at the Real Organic Food Project Annual Meeting in New York
| | | Dear friends,I've been teaching a class called _The Law of Democracy_ for over a decade now, using a casebook that is taught around the country. As the whole structure of government teeters, and people have been disgusted with corruption for decades, it can feel a little strange, even grandiose, to call the laws governing American political processes "the law of democracy."Whatever it is that we have now, there is a temptation to act, act, and act more, or at least engage in the simulacrum of acting (tweeting and moaning). But what we do need to be doing is thinking hard, coming together and really diagnosing what it would take for people to govern themselves. That means going beyond the daily yelling about Trump or "how Democrats can win," and instead grappling with deeper questions of structure, power, and possibility. Last week, I had really interesting conversations about power and democracy at [the Real Organic Project convening ](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/7c0b77f5?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)that happens every year in a round barn in Columbia County (with [Marion Nestle](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/295a1611?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21), my favorite blueberry farmer [Hugh Kent](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/1aba2f52?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21), and legend [Eliot Coleman](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/9ed30f3a?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)). But in my day job, I'm organizing a Fordham Law Review Symposium, _" Antitrust Law and Oligarchy: The Intersection of Markets, Democracy, and Power,"_ which will take place on **Friday, October 10, 2025** , at Fordham Law School. The event is free and open to the public, but registration is required. You can register [here.](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/62670874?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21) It's 9 AM to 4 PM. It's going to be an interesting day. The opening panel will bring together Helene Landemore, who writes about how we can select more representatives by lottery, instead of elections, as an anti-corruption tool with Guy Uriel-Charles, who writes about race and entrenchment and the risks of populism with Luigi Zingales, who argues that concentrated power is wholly incompatible with self-rule, and Ganesh Sitaramen, who argues that public utility law is a way to protect against oligarchy. They are going to disagree with each other, and we need to hear those disagreements. Later in the day, there's a fireside **Lina Khan** , who, as chair of the FTC, demanded that we recognize the democratic role of antimonopoly traditions, will be in conversation with my colleague Oliver Sylvain, reflecting on the challenges of antitrust enforcement in this extraordinary moment of concentrated corporate power.The New York Lieutenant Governor Antonio Delgado will be our keynote--he's been on fire recently slamming the failures of the current ways of thinking about economic and political power. [ The Fordham Law Review presents Antitrust Law and Oligarchy: The Intersection of Markets, Democracy, and Power Friday, October 10, 2025 8:30 - 9:00 a.m. | check-in 9:00 a.m. - 4:30 p.m. | program (subject to change) In-Person and ... Google Docs ](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/7b5757a1?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21) Other people on the panel include: Shaoul Sussman, Doha Mekki, John Newman, Sarah Bauerle Danzman, Theodosia Stavroulaki, Hayden Rooke-Ley, and more. These are regulators, policymakers, scholars who have thought about how to break up big medicine and the relationship between platform power and democracy. Here's the [full description](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/fded5c90?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21):_This symposium will explore how the concentration of economic power directly shapes political society and democratic (or oligarchic) governance. As regulatory bodies, legal scholars, and policymakers grapple with how different kinds of structural reforms can address political oligarchy, this symposium will provide a critical forum for discussion. It will examine the relationship between the antimonopoly tradition and other arenas of democratic reform, consider how monopoly power in technology and health platforms affects speech, political power, and public accountability, and analyze how enforcement agencies, such as the DOJ and FTC, are confronting the challenges of oligarchic power in the modern era. This interdisciplinary symposium will bring together leading legal theorists, policymakers, and enforcers to assess evolving legal strategies for countering oligarchic structures._ Whether or not you can make it, I want to hear your ideas about the fundamental rules that govern our food system, media, AI, voting, how we make decisions. What can law do about oligarchy? What laws have enabled the current mess, and what can we do about them?See you there and [support this newsletter](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/9bd18fd9?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21) if you can. Warmly,
ZephyrPS: Can you imagine if we decided that instead of building AI data centers we should be building systems to support local farming and healthy food? You know that Larry Ellison has a half a billion dollar AI farming project on his monopolized island? And that Bill Gates owns more farmland than anyone? And that Walmart, the largest grocer in the United States, is also one of the biggest players in AI? Big Grocery is building "smart stores" with Microsoft and Nvidia, using AI to monitor shelves, adjust prices, and Nestle and Pepsi are embedding AI in everything from marketing to "personalized nutrition." Meanwhile Sysco is partnering with Amazon's AWS and ADM has joined hands with Microsoft to apply AI across its supply chain. So don't let anyone disconnect the food story from the AI story. Big centralized AI is deep inside the systems that run our food.
---

---

name: Winsome
subject: Patriot – Slaves did not die in the fields so that we could call ourselves victims now in 2025
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Winsome for Governor
body:

At the same time, Democrats are seeing a surge of enthusiasm from their radical base. The Left’s willingness to shut down the government just shows how far they’re willing to go to force their agenda onto the American people. With our race already a major target for Democrat activists, it’s not enough to barely hit our fundraising targets. We need to come out of the gates for our final quarter of 2025 on 🔥. Democrats think they have all the momentum at the moment. If our fundraising hits the gas in these first 72 hours of the quarter, we’ll erase any momentum Democrats think they have. name: Forward Party
subject: Forward Stands for Solutions, Not Stalemates
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Forward Party and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
Here's what's happening in the Forward world...

| | [ ](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=JgyyvCfQwldlSeD7KJl_Ih7269o_4hwqOhPs1_PHvgI&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_80b&n=1)
---
Thursday, October 02, 2025 | Peter -- Forward Stands for Solutions, Not Stalemates! Forward believes our federal budget and spending bills should be produced through regular order with debate and compromise, not by holding the country hostage. With Forward on the ballot, voters can demand leaders who do their jobs, put people first, and make government work again. Here is our official statement on this shutdown. This week, we also issued an endorsement of Danny Ceisler for Bucks County Sheriff. Danny is a Bronze Star recipient who served as a U.S. Army intelligence officer, deployed to Afghanistan, and later worked on the COVID Crisis Management Team at the Pentagon. He earned his law degree from Temple University and has served as both a prosecutor and a civil attorney for victims of crime, police officers, and veterans. He also co-owns a local pub and brewery, volunteers as a firefighter, and has worked as a senior public safety official in Gov. Josh Shapiro’s administration. To read more about Danny, click [here. ](https://www.forwardparty.com/media/blog/forward-party-endorses-danny-ceisler-for-bucks-county-sheriff/?e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_80b&n=2) [](https://www.forwardparty.com/media/blog/forward-party-endorses-danny-ceisler-for-bucks-county-sheriff/?e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_80b&n=3) 👉 Check out the growing list of endorsed candidates here:[ forwardparty.com/candidates](https://www.forwardparty.com/candidates?e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_80b&n=4) In addition, Forward issued [this statement ](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=mhUdGCm_eEqxEEjcw0-lGbIN6AZpbu4iFxE-qCh2CjFCwCLUGiG0VSo5ciyV2qXL4v1U5z5FjRuyWcPNgx3FqA&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_80b&n=5)because we believe public discourse should be based on respect, decency, and accountability – not personal attacks or dehumanizing rhetoric. All Forward candidates commit to a pledge to avoid personal threats or vilification, reinforcing our value of principled political behavior. By speaking out, we underscore that real reform starts with how we treat one another and that toxic rhetoric has no place in politics and government.
**[Forward CEO Lindsey Drath Joined Fox 5 D.C. to discuss the government shutdown ](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=DkTthycDobhXxHMBv-XLRV-DHAXER-yfy5V6_udw_fXpnqz8YlftC-7Wbvtcn19_&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_80b&n=6)| Fox 5** On the day the government shutdown due to a funding standoff in Congress, Forward CEO Lindsey Drath joined Fox 5 D.C. to explain how this type of disfunction hurts everyone. [**Forward Party’s LT Governor Kerry Healey****Joined ABC 7 to discuss the government shutdown**](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=_BNtuvNT9V_JqWn2w6nllz3TkmyYu6I8O8EGORZNkqBk9zop0agBxpaoXIS7XWeg&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_80b&n=7)**[](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=_BNtuvNT9V_JqWn2w6nllz3TkmyYu6I8O8EGORZNkqBk9zop0agBxpaoXIS7XWeg&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_80b&n=8)**| ABC 7**** On the same day the government shutdown due to a funding standoff in Congress, Forward Executive Director Lieutenant Governor Kerry Healey joined ABC 7 to explain how Congressional disfunction lead us to this moment. [Danielle Chesebrough joined Brian and Company ](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=5DyRE-n-v5JBXPRw24Bn1JoG5ANrc9IpBucQVmjX0YwONv7kEy2raoI00DFKi9BTdQVrjk6WRzeqxTULzuo2XriR3o-_zye7Oa3OIFru880QTVsydKDUQzrsMddRC3_cEPH1rz9orkQbggMYY6kD9w&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_80b&n=9) **| Brian and Company** First Selectwoman of Stonington, CT and Chair of Connecticut Forward Danielle Chesebrough discusses the division in politics and how the two parties could come together.
**October 7 | National Volunteer Program Kickoff Party!** The National Forward Party is launching a volunteer program! If you're in a state that doesn't have active leadership yet, or you're just interested in honing your volunteer skills, look no further! We are going to host a kickoff party where we share all the ways to get engaged with us, and give away a little merch. More information and registration [here. ](https://home.forwardparty.com/nvp_kickoff_party?e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_80b&n=10)
[**See the five types of independents that have big implications for American politics** ](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=197AKNOSY8NsJuieaSWzPXF8S5ivb07I4TyhNYi_te3-JEyXfQjxMOreQEfEt0LAnzejlRuVX5DAykaSn0VZKWAtuSxYFq_TUa5GZgYMPRhodo2aVCuAMzipHA_BjvgH&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_80b&n=11)| **CNN** A new CNN poll takes a deeper look into who political independents are finds the notion of a classic swing voter is a thing of the past. **[Most voters think America's divisions cannot be overcome](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=ZECkydug-5JCfUQCPK7zbHNGq15Zen2GKPE6oWRfQSM6qleOkTnuE_6laAtUoD_4mXQrI-TPFB7N_qRuxCqx5K8XqPlsEDIiqPlh2f4NzhDACN_cmJA8z6rxZaIkHl4dl9OisU6JDe8vW7icPaIwXV2ejPqm_9EPGcmjWk9aC7cSAXsqXmCtYTe6KAFA4BhW&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_80b&n=12) **| **New York Times** A new Times/Siena survey shows a significant shift among voters, as their concerns about the health of the political system overtake other issues.
[ ](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=HN-VuZ6-MtyTZDOwpLeQohBI2w5n1fstLPCAOB1eS8pn49pgKy1KM2eEYboZkJaE&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_80b&n=13) [ ](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=HR9MoATtHgNjF_4kmlo5pN8R4zJ-fGDLgTAMST8PY6mgKwzOK4bTDEpy9KU5BOzg&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_80b&n=14) [ ](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=C67zBN9acu3ZSVmLPV47peHvpkNPm4HcBUGM4-0XJTPgmEVLc_dN12xbGWLIkZIR&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_80b&n=15) [ ](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=KO8ZOB0mq2gq63DvFrKfe08uhOv-byHfKpIIFrTtOpPikh4D0waR8pVrn6wFcdzO&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_80b&n=16) [ ](https://home.forwardparty.com/r?u=_KeSraPW0gSlxEBMOvJrEuogCR1FcLzdL_LSGKjfikF5BT1dZn3HN2gM4OEGEI0z&e=61868e7acdd36ae0ddf3e39aa8429171&utm_source=forwardparty&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fast_forward_80b&n=17) Copyright © 2025 Forward Party, all rights reserved. Forward believes our federal budget and spending bills should be produced through regular order with debate and compromise, not by holding the country hostage. name: Russell Cleveland: Democratic Candidate for MT D-1
subject: Unopposed as Democratic Choice for U.S. House D-1
body:
Russell is putting in the work while others sit back and talk about potentially doing the work!

| | | | | |
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| | | | | |
**171 Days on the Road for Montana**
---
| | | | | |
As we step into October, I want to share an update from the campaign trail.
Russell Cleveland has now run **unopposed for 170 days** in Montana’s District 1. That’s 170 days of shaking hands, listening to neighbors, and putting in the miles. In fact, he’s already logged the equivalent of driving the entire length of the Earth’s equator to meet people across this district.
Why? Because **he cares**. Because every community, from the Bitterroot to the Flathead and from Butte to the Hi-Line, deserves to be heard.
This campaign isn’t about political extremes. It’s about showing up, listening, and delivering Montana solutions for Montana families. And Russ is proving that every day.
We’re building momentum together and we can’t do it without you. We thank you for the support thus far and are relying on your continued support for the next 171 Days. If you’re able, please [**donate to the campaign**](https://russellcleveland.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b7cef8d6a1da540dae5618949&id=6df01343ed&e=375cee343b)**** or [**offer an endorsement**](https://russellcleveland.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b7cef8d6a1da540dae5618949&id=f678ee6419&e=375cee343b)**.**
Onward,
Team Cleveland

name: Brian Bengs
subject: I’m running
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Brian Bengs for South Dakota
body:
I left the National Parks to run for Senate

| [|
---](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/Klw2AJGulj3kbW_jpPBPxQ~~/AAUS1hA~/akdtGhlYeQVgy1aKGAmBtaut_9jriiTUX5Pu274m9AeQYL56QO2y2PScO1S8nKTYv-bOLLAYBLI2nv5L9NNDYh8-XZSFBEGa6bpyp7p-Jf-zvjGP-xxKjHXGyltxRL8K)
| | | | Peter, My name is Brian Bengs. I’m a retired Air Force Lieutenant Colonel, a JAG officer, and until recently, I wore the uniform of a Wind Cave National Park Ranger. I am running to represent South Dakota in the United States Senate as an Independent because I believe in the Cowboy Code of the West: **tell the truth and take care of your neighbor.** The modern U.S. Senate is a country club. It’s full of career politicians like Mike Rounds, who answer only to corporate PACs and D.C. party bosses. They have no idea what it’s like to work the night shift, pay for feed, **or worry about a government shutdown cutting off essential services.** [**I’m asking you to join me in changing that by becoming a Founding Donor to my campaign for Senate today.**](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/frDCXR5Cv2ZwAN-xF1MP8w~~/AAUS1hA~/ROOct11UgbnMGgdIwcqI3hYKWxDaM61_98uK7aKpD4AR2JKZdgpFKKg7nqsqckdVHPc_nnav9sftOHiTgxdO8nD4Ku6G39efMo1QT5FB2-r1Xsls1CD0ToIu1_RPx4OIlMH12hbhtSSujIn_Fzph2tlgKbkAg11h4d6NiwKhXuEjEAGWYsWF1jt0ioFgBR9BEYeN64i92PCjQ2TX0K7Q54a6gvRVSaxBMSYTUZHzf6ofG8NlO1F4enwa0-adbPrulQvyqG1HzVX_cDGni6-X9Q~~) These guys have become far more comfortable in D.C. than in the states that elected them. They believe their wealth and loyalty to the party exempts them from the basic principles of service and integrity. That’s why we have chaos in our country. That’s why places like South Dakota get ignored. I’ve served this country for 26 years in the military. I don't answer to a political party or a single personality because I swore an oath to the Constitution. But taking on millions in corporate money and a D.C. machine requires a team built on principles, not privilege. It requires people like you. Listen, I know you can’t donate to every race, every organization, or every email. **But, here’s why I’d like you to consider giving to our campaign:** Mike Rounds has a 1 _7% approval rating_ among independent voters in South Dakota, and your dollars go further in our race because the cost of media and campaign resources is significantly lower here. **In fact, a $100 donation to my campaign is equivalent to a $3,100 contribution in Florida.** [**If you believe South Dakota deserves a Senator who values integrity and service over self, will you make your first contribution today?**](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/frDCXR5Cv2ZwAN-xF1MP8w~~/AAUS1hA~/ROOct11UgbnMGgdIwcqI3hYKWxDaM61_98uK7aKpD4AR2JKZdgpFKKg7nqsqckdVHPc_nnav9sftOHiTgxdO8nD4Ku6G39efMo1QT5FB2-r1Xsls1CD0ToIu1_RPx4OIlMH12hbhtSSujIn_Fzph2tlgKbkAg11h4d6NiwKhXuEjEAGWYsWF1jt0ioFgBR9BEYeN64i92PCjQ2TX0K7Q54a6gvRVSaxBMSYTUZHzf6ofG8NlO1F4enwa0-adbPrulQvyqG1HzVX_cDGni6-X9Q~~) **_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_** **[**DONATE $5 »** clean_body: I left the National Parks to run for Senate | | | | | Peter, My name is Brian Bengs. I’m a retired Air Force Lieutenant Colonel, a JAG officer, and until recently, I wore the uniform of a Wind Cave National Park Ranger. I am running to represent South Dakota in the United States Senate as an Independent because I believe in the Cowboy Code of the West: tell the truth and take care of your neighbor. The modern U.S. Senate is a country club. It’s full of career politicians like Mike Rounds, who answer only to corporate PACs and D.C. party bosses. They have no idea what it’s like to work the night shift, pay for feed, or worry about a government shutdown cutting off essential services. ~~ These guys have become far more comfortable in D.C. than in the states that elected them. They believe their wealth and loyalty to the party exempts them from the basic principles of service and integrity. That’s why we have chaos in our country. That’s why places like South Dakota get ignored. I’ve served this country for 26 years in the military. I don't answer to a political party or a single personality because I swore an oath to the Constitution. But taking on millions in corporate money and a D.C. machine requires a team built on principles, not privilege. It requires people like you. Listen, I know you can’t donate to every race, every organization, or every email. But, here’s why I’d like you to consider giving to our campaign: Mike Rounds has a 1 7% approval rating among independent voters in South Dakota, and your dollars go further in our race because the cost of media and campaign resources is significantly lower here. In fact, a $100 donation to my campaign is equivalent to a $3,100 contribution in Florida. ~~ If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~~ We should fund service. Not the swamp. Thank you for being part of the team that’s ready to shock Washington. Brian Bengs Independent Candidate for U.S. Senate ~ ~ ** ** | Paid for by Brian Bengs for South Dakota Brian Bengs for South DakotaPO Box 404Hot Springs, SD 57747United States "Website"

body:

Peter,
Here’s the truth: **We missed our end-of-quarter fundraising goal.**
We didn’t fall short by much. But in a race this competitive, every single dollar matters.
Here’s why it’s so important we close the gap and get back on track today:
* Campaigning in a state as large as California takes serious resources. Your contribution will help us reach voters from Chico to San Diego.
* Polls show our campaign is leading—and we can’t afford to lose this critical early momentum.
* Lots of voters are still undecided. If we’re the campaign that reaches them first, we could win them over and grow our lead.

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 10/2/2025
body:
The dangerous gentleman enters the race: Victor Marx launches gubernatorial bid
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
| | Hi friends,
🎙️ Check out today's Mountain Minute podcast [here](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), or watch on [YouTube](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) or [Rumble](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)! Here is a summary of today's top stories:
🏔️ **Here in Colorado...** A state review found widespread [misconduct in juvenile detention centers](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), exposing over 1,000 strip searches that violated policy and raised alarms about oversight. [CU Boulder was fined $50,000](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) after fans chanted anti-LDS slurs at a BYU game, drawing apologies from leaders. Meanwhile, RTD is seeking [$1.6 billion for rail expansion](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) even as Denver faces budget strains, while a study shows [relocating the USDA to Fort Collins](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) could bring 6,000 jobs and $1 billion in output. Denver Public Schools may [pursue another bond](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) despite voters approving $975 million last year.
**🇺🇸 On the National Stage...** Congress’s failure to strike a deal has triggered a [partial government shutdown](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), furloughing federal workers and disrupting agencies. At the same time, Secretary of War Pete Hegseth vowed to restore strength and standards to the military, promising to [cut “wokeness” and refocus on readiness](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), discipline, and victory.
🗣️ **Commentary Spotlight...** Sean Bateman warns that Charlie Kirk’s assassination underscored a [larger ideological battle](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) between truth and relativism, while Laureen Boll points to Colorado schools, where [unions push ideology over parental authority](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.). Both argue the fight for objective truth—whether in culture, politics, or classrooms—is central to preserving freedom and America’s future.
This is the Mountain Minute for Thursday, October 2, 2025.
– Team RMV
---
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[The dangerous gentleman enters the race: Victor Marx launches gubernatorial bid](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
The crowd inside Phil Long Music Hall stood and cheered as Victor Marx made it official. On Oct. 1, the Marine veteran and founder of All Things Possible Ministries announced his run for Colorado governor, joined by local pastors, veterans and elected officials—backing his call to restore justice and public safety.
Supporters throughout the night echoed a recurring phrase—“dangerous gentleman”—a term used to describe Marx’s combination of restraint, conviction and action.
A survivor of childhood abuse and a Marine Corps veteran, Marx has spent the last two decades leading international rescue missions through his nonprofit. “I’ve spent my life fighting battles most politicians wouldn’t dare face,” he said at the event.
Founded in 2003, All Things Possible Ministries specializes in high-risk trauma intervention, including work with child abuse victims, war-zone rescues and law enforcement agencies across the U.S. and abroad.
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[“They don’t care”: Unleashed podcast spotlights Durango parents’ loss of trust](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Heidi Ganahl’s Unleashed podcast spotlighted Durango parents’ frustration with the school board, featuring a father and a charter school advocate who said leaders ignore parent concerns, waste money on DEI, and sideline families on key decisions.
* Parents cited issues such as gender bathroom policies, lack of transparency, and political favoritism, including the board being fined for promoting a member’s State House run with school resources.
* Charter school efforts were blocked despite strong demand, leaving families with few options, while Ganahl urged parents to demand clarity, accountability, and greater involvement in school decisions.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
|
---
**[Beyond the rhetoric: Schools, unions, and the battle for objective truth in education](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY LAUREEN BOLL, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Laureen Boll argues Colorado’s upcoming school board elections will determine whether parents or progressive agendas drive education policy.
* She highlights DCSD’s new opt-in policy for the Healthy Kids Colorado Survey as a win for parental authority, contrasting “Common Sense” candidates’ defense of parents with progressives’ focus on data collection.
* Boll warns that unions and progressive candidates blur truth with ideology, pushing activism over academics, while Common Sense candidates emphasize objective truth, fiscal responsibility, and respect for parents.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
** ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️ **
**[Is this the War for the West?](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY SEAN BATEMAN | COMMENTARY | OCTOBER 1** _It has now been just over two weeks since Charlie Kirk was assassinated, and the articles, news stories, podcasts, and posts have not slowed down. The reactions have ranged far and wide, but the mainstream narratives have bifurcated into the Left asking to “tone down the rhetoric” and the Right discussing how this was “the crossing of the Rubicon,” “the shot heard round the world,” or the start of “war."_
__
**[Denver’s Spending Grew 60% in a Decade, But Revenues and Population Lagged Far Behind](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**BY SHAINA COLE | LOCAL | SEPTEMBER 30** _Denver’s budget has grown sharply over the past ten years, far faster than its population and its ability to bring in tax dollars. A new analysis from the Common Sense Institute shows inflation-adjusted spending per resident rose about 60 percent since 2015. During that period, revenues grew more slowly, at about 40 percent, while the city’s population increased by less than 7 percent._
__

**[When will the health-related employment bubble burst?](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY MIKE O'DONNELL | COMMENTARY | SEPTEMBER 30** _A few weeks ago, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) announced that it had inadvertently overstated—by more than 100%—the number of new jobs created across the United States during the last full year of the Biden/Harris Administration._
__

**Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share?** We'd love to talk with you about becoming an RMV Contributor. Reply to this email (or reach out to [tori@rockymountainvoice.com](mailto:tori@rockymountainvoice.com?subject=Contributor%20Interest%20\(via%20Mountain%20Minute\))) or send us a message on social media to chat
|
---
|
---
**📲 DID YOU KNOW?** **Apple was founded on April Fools’ Day, 1976** , by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne. Just 12 days later, Wayne sold his 10% share for $800—missing out on billions as Apple grew into the first U.S. company worth $1 trillion in 2018.
|
---
**CARTOON OF THE DAY**
|
---
**QUOTE OF THE DAY** "Sell not virtue to purchase wealth, nor liberty to purchase power." – Benjamin Franklin

BY SHAINA COLE | LOCAL | SEPTEMBER 30 Denver’s budget has grown sharply over the past ten years, far faster than its population and its ability to bring in tax dollars. A new analysis from the Common Sense Institute shows inflation-adjusted spending per resident rose about 60 percent since 2015. During that period, revenues grew more slowly, at about 40 percent, while the city’s population increased by less than 7 percent. MIKE O'DONNELL | COMMENTARY | SEPTEMBER 30 A few weeks ago, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) announced that it had inadvertently overstated—by more than 100%—the number of new jobs created across the United States during the last full year of the Biden/Harris Administration. __ Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share? We'd love to talk with you about becoming an RMV Contributor. Apple was founded on April Fools’ Day, 1976 , by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne. Just 12 days later, Wayne sold his 10% share for $800—missing out on billions as Apple grew into the first U.S. company worth $1 trillion in 2018. | CARTOON OF THE DAY | QUOTE OF THE DAY "Sell not virtue to purchase wealth, nor liberty to purchase power." – Benjamin Franklin | | | | ---|---|--- Did a friend forward you this email? | A product of Rocky Mountain Voice, Inc. 8200 S Quebec St, STE A3601 Centennial, CO 80112 This message was originally sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com Be sure to put “RockyMountainVoice.com” on your safe-senders whitelist. Peter,

The partisan divide gripping our nation has turned Washington into a warzone of its own—fierce, unrelenting, and filled with entrenched elites more interested in power than progress.

_**As a Navy SEAL, I've faced harsh environments and impossible odds, from battlefields abroad to the political arena at home. Those experiences taught me how to stay calm under pressure, broker compromise, and accomplish the mission—no matter the stakes.**_

I'm Sen. Tim Sheehy, and I'm in the U.S. Senate to serve you, not the special interests or partisan extremists. The liberal media and their allies attack me for my resolve to challenge the status quo and expose the waste and corruption draining our nation. They fear leaders who put America first and refuse to bow to their agenda.

My SEAL background gives me a unique perspective:**I know how to navigate chaos, build bridges, and lower temperatures in even the most hostile settings.** In Congress, I'm fighting for practical solutions—securing our borders, strengthening our economy, and restoring accountability in government.

**But I can't do it alone.**

Democrats are outraising us, and they're voting in elections that, to put it simply, we're not voting in. Recently, voter complacency in Iowa cost Republicans a super-majority in the Iowa Senate.

What happens when that complacency extends to U.S. House and Senate races?

We lose.

I can't abide by that - and I don't believe you can either - _**so I'm sounding the alarm right now.**_

Peter, I need your support to keep pushing for a stronger, united America. Together, we can reject the elites' divisive tactics and build a future that honors the sacrifices of our forefathers.

subject: Democrats are standing up for you and your family’s health care
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
It happened. The federal government is shut down.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116468765/573520075/150430950?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3Yjg3NGYwZi1kNDlmLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=a3gx-70azUh5ZpZAilMSnVbT8uBWB2oIZFsqp2oDwtw=&emci=c6cae821-bb9f-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=7b874f0f-d49f-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=7731215)
---

| | It happened. The federal government is shut down. Trump and Republicans have shut down the government because they want to continue to attack health care for people across America. Republicans’ refusal to extend the tax credits that help people afford health care will cause a massive increase in health care costs for Americans. According to the nonpartisan Kaiser Family Foundation, on average, they're expecting premium payments for people who don't get health insurance through their job or a public program like Medicare or Medicaid — which is millions of Americans — **to increase by 114% if Republicans refuse to extend these credits.** This has huge impacts:
* Working people will have to make impossible choices between affording health care or putting food on the table
* People will avoid getting care, and small problems will become big problems
* Health care will overall become more expensive for every American

And that's not all. In January 2027 — so Republicans can avoid any electoral consequences in the 2026 midterms — the devastating Medicaid cuts from Republicans' One Big Beautiful Betrayal start. Millions of Americans will lose their health coverage. To add fuel to this fire, Trump's team doesn't respect the rule of law. They're canceling contracts, slow walking or freezing funds, defunding programs they don't like, and blatantly ignoring the Constitution. SCOTUS has clearly said a line-item veto is a violation of the Constitution. These funding actions essentially achieve the same strategy, giving the president the power to pick and choose. This is all part of Trump's plan to consolidate and shift power from legislators to the president. And that's why any funding bill we pass has to have safeguards that tell the president that laws are not suggestions. This is a monumentally important fight. There is an authoritarian takeover, and the president is grabbing the power of the purse at the same time. Trump's attacks have included:
* Stifling free speech
* Weaponizing the Department of Justice
* Blackmailing universities and law firms to alter their advocacy in a free society
* And now, he's making the military accustomed to depressing dissent in U.S. cities, which violates the law that only allows the military to be deployed domestically in an invasion or rebellion — setting the stage to allow future use of military force, including perhaps, during protests related to the next election

This fight is incredibly important, and the time to fight is now. We need to give the people someone or something to rally around. In this critical moment, I need your backing. Please generously help everyone who is willing to stand up, fight back against the authoritarian takeover, and fight for the fundamentals families need to thrive. Onward, _Jeff_ | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution to Jeff Merkley instantly:

name: Derek Tran
subject: I came to Congress to serve, not play political games.
body:
|
#
Thank you, friend. **We’re still tallying the numbers, but we can say this: With your support, we’re on track to post our strongest fundraising quarter of the year. ** That kind of momentum doesn’t happen without people like you stepping up. I don’t take it for granted, and it’s proof we’re building real momentum together, the kind that can’t be bought. Of course there’s no time to celebrate. Right as we closed the books on the deadline, House Republicans triggered a government shutdown. This may seem like more of the same political theater from Republicans, but the consequences of this stunt are very real:
* Service members will go without pay
* Law enforcement and federal workers face mass furloughs and firings
* Programs working families depend on are at risk of being defunded

Meanwhile, the White House ballroom construction is still full steam ahead. Don’t say you honor the troops while you fund gold chandeliers. How about funding affordable healthcare instead? Republicans are more interested in chaos than solutions. They’re choosing to hurt people rather than work on bipartisan answers. I came to Congress to serve people, not play political games. That’s the contrast: while they sow chaos, we’re building strength. I’m calling out the lies and holding Republicans accountable – because working families in CA-45 and beyond deserve better. Thank you, Derek
---

name: Chip Roy
subject: It's time to honor the cry for freedom

Texas is under assault, Fellow American.

From open-border politicians, radical leftists, and faceless foreign corporations that threaten our sovereignty, safety, and our way of life, **it's time to draw a line in the sand.**

That's why I am running for Attorney General - to protect our Texas way of life.

For so long, Texas was a conservative beacon. We have a proud tradition of rising to defend our homes, our freedoms, and our communities. Texas is anchored by liberty, freedom, and self-determination.

But radical Leftists moving here are threatening to turn Texas blue. Politicians like Colin Allred are flooding our state with Hollywood and Wall Street donors.

_**They will do whatever they can to turn Texas into California or New York.**_

name: Jane Fonda
subject: I sat down with the LA Times
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee
body:
Here's what I told them.

| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/116468933/573520676/1328794144?refcode=ea.5db40f09-d59f-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ms=ea.5db40f09-d59f-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjVkYjQwZjA5LWQ1OWYtZjAxMS04ZTYxLTYwNDViZGVkOGJhNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=RWk5PNVcpQ222IiMkIngPh9F71nuRkrppv_oF_JmTTk=&emci=73fc163b-b29f-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=5db40f09-d59f-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=4823105)
---
I recently had the privilege to sit down with the _Los Angeles Times_ to talk about the climate crisis, my journey through activism, and why the work we're doing at JanePAC matters more than ever. I'd love to share some of my thoughts with you. [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/116468934/573520677/2105508926?refcode=ea.5db40f09-d59f-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ms=ea.5db40f09-d59f-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjVkYjQwZjA5LWQ1OWYtZjAxMS04ZTYxLTYwNDViZGVkOGJhNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=RWk5PNVcpQ222IiMkIngPh9F71nuRkrppv_oF_JmTTk=)_Photo by Christina House / Los Angeles Times_ **I’ve always believed that if you want to go fast, go alone. If you want to go far, go together.** My path to founding JanePAC wouldn’t have been possible without grassroots supporters like you. It started in 2019 when Greta Thunberg and the youth climate movement inspired me to step up. She asked, “Where are the older people?” So I showed up. I helped launch Fire Drill Fridays. I protested, I lobbied, and I got arrested. But it still wasn’t enough. Too many politicians — Democrats and Republicans — take money from the fossil fuel lobby. That’s when I realized that to truly move the needle, we needed more than passion. We needed political power. That’s why I started JanePAC. If you can’t change the people, __change__ the people. We focused our efforts (_and we still do_) on the critical races that rarely make headlines: state legislatures, city councils, school boards, utility commissions. These down-ballot races fly under the radar, but they have the biggest impact on climate policy and the communities on the frontlines of this crisis. And that's when politicians started listening. They saw the power of our grassroots organizing. They saw supporters who refuse to accept the climate crisis as someone else's problem. Since 2022, we've helped elect over 160 climate champions nationwide. In 2024 alone, we supported 154 campaigns and won 96 races. This year, we already have 7 victories under our belt. And we're just getting started. Trump and his allies are attacking our democracy and our climate at the same time. But here's what they don't understand: _we're not going anywhere._ I'm 87 years old, and this is what I'm going to be doing for the rest of my life. I refuse to stand by while our planet burns. And when I look at this movement we've built together, I know we have the power to win. Thank you for being part of this movement. xx Jane [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/116468935/573520678/2105508926?refcode=ea.5db40f09-d59f-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ms=ea.5db40f09-d59f-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjVkYjQwZjA5LWQ1OWYtZjAxMS04ZTYxLTYwNDViZGVkOGJhNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=RWk5PNVcpQ222IiMkIngPh9F71nuRkrppv_oF_JmTTk=) P.S. clean_body: Here's what I told them. | I recently had the privilege to sit down with the Los Angeles Times to talk about the climate crisis, my journey through activism, and why the work we're doing at JanePAC matters more than ever. I'd love to share some of my thoughts with you. by Christina House / Los Angeles Times I’ve always believed that if you want to go fast, go alone. If you want to go far, go together. My path to founding JanePAC wouldn’t have been possible without grassroots supporters like you. It started in 2019 when Greta Thunberg and the youth climate movement inspired me to step up. She asked, “Where are the older people?” So I showed up. I helped launch Fire Drill Fridays. I protested, I lobbied, and I got arrested. But it still wasn’t enough. Too many politicians — Democrats and Republicans — take money from the fossil fuel lobby. That’s when I realized that to truly move the needle, we needed more than passion. We needed political power. That’s why I started JanePAC. If you can’t change the people, change the people. We focused our efforts (and we still do) on the critical races that rarely make headlines: state legislatures, city councils, school boards, utility commissions. These down-ballot races fly under the radar, but they have the biggest impact on climate policy and the communities on the frontlines of this crisis. And that's when politicians started listening. They saw the power of our grassroots organizing. They saw supporters who refuse to accept the climate crisis as someone else's problem. Since 2022, we've helped elect over 160 climate champions nationwide. In 2024 alone, we supported 154 campaigns and won 96 races. This year, we already have 7 victories under our belt. And we're just getting started. Trump and his allies are attacking our democracy and our climate at the same time. But here's what they don't understand: we're not going anywhere. I'm 87 years old, and this is what I'm going to be doing for the rest of my life. I refuse to stand by while our planet burns. And when I look at this movement we've built together, I know we have the power to win. Thank you for being part of this movement. xx Jane = P.S. name: Adam Schwarze
subject: Democrats just shut down our government
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Schwarze for Senate
body:
Angie Craig and Senate Democrats are putting you last. |
---

I am livid. And I am sure it makes you mad, too, Fellow Conservative.

The Democrats Shutdown the Government!

_**Chuck Schumer and Senate Democrats voted for illegal immigrants over our veterans. **_

_**Our active duty military must go without pay.**_

_**Our veterans won't have the benefits they deserve because of Schumer's shutdown.**_

All because they want free healthcare for illegal immigrants.

Are you mad yet?

What's worse is that one of my Democrat opponents, Angie Craig, also voted against this same CR to fund the government in the House, even after she called previous government shutdowns "_putting politics above people_." _What is she doing now?!_

Fellow Conservative, we cannot allow another Schumer lackey to vote against President Trump's America First Agenda - _AND YOU._

**[That's why I need you on my team to help take on Angie Craig, Chuck Schumer, and these shutdown Democrats.](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=e138796d05ab47c58cb84986d7188526&_e=jaZbM8KMRuD-LPCTQ1BAs1JW0Z-WMsy9ByVK_Y6YxpYDLWMSBSi5iYfkWCdK-P7994HbN81mmvYT0L3qTs5DQ9ickWTx0xdHzXFYLen8JX8VLkbkbyE9OndOvAP6gZd8ycAfsotVx_b7mRTij4esqC05jrOimjcXh5LygAs6Ck86Y7DVyKTeqZQyxGdUbiZFId0JyGu6o1qijA6hm50BYkHp5fKsBtJl1h2Hlp1ULuGWt5KzQ43ELt8frbcG25vbZ6NqERmvVH_IBKKswwgc3ExJlki99WZf7A9Bp58MZnYqC6Ih2r98jFyAxE0662pERrvPad4o2ApIIkhtAXMtzS58RHnie1V5_0x9bRk_5hRE7kal_ap-_tUmXx1iO2ATqWEg52OeObfTR9T0UMpztRgekUcVLxPFAj1WeohvyIZPDXiqYvikKbejSzmuK3KrVBKEOgLFAE4I0v-dipnlWUEtPV95eCOqyP2lNdWvI5i-aBXIuhfKs53M5SpIrW_jz_sQarzG8k63sqol47LC1SDOdPu0gKlo21zXeSp8p6s3rWrnHbw3Q39kjy4RMRcy4C_sXBm8VKNthByrzKDmey6PafKIE1CymwLDWPpjs-MGdT9QU4Ch4TgFPQDsNpl3O2tXdswFQq7z0WMTZhvyllS2pL_Z5L86GcikfoMmwKZaFJI7jmV6X77bS2oBS2CkrTdvDi1sdhqwsDIzg46MjBVtGrSZf-LHQqIfvbqNFWkMpy9pHjUlnLyB13Q2UCjcYbKEUzFCfbPupoEuB8mySA%3D%3D)**

I'm not a career politician. I'm a regular guy from a working-class family who grew up playing hockey and answered our nation's call on my 18th birthday in October 2001, just weeks after the 9/11 attacks.

I joined the Marine Corps to defend our country, and as a Navy SEAL, I deployed nine times to over 70 countries, to protect our freedoms and way of life - and it's unthinkable that the men and women who put their lives on the line will go without pay and the benefits they risked their lives to earn.

I don't have the funding that my Democrat opponents do - they have the backing of Tim Walz, National Democrats, and the liberal donor base. **[That's why I am counting on you.](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=e138796d05ab47c58cb84986d7188526&_e=jaZbM8KMRuD-LPCTQ1BAs1JW0Z-WMsy9ByVK_Y6YxpYDLWMSBSi5iYfkWCdK-P7994HbN81mmvYT0L3qTs5DQ9ickWTx0xdHzXFYLen8JX8VLkbkbyE9OndOvAP6gZd8ycAfsotVx_b7mRTij4esqC05jrOimjcXh5LygAs6Ck86Y7DVyKTeqZQyxGdUbiZFId0JyGu6o1qijA6hm50BYkHp5fKsBtJl1h2Hlp1ULuGWt5KzQ43ELt8frbcG25vbZ6NqERmvVH_IBKKswwgc3CgCSFkL_KQt3B-kAti7_ZlTSs0HRn8Z9a36DXCIzkIoFTx9Qrw-Gl7jPv5hqqDrBghSdQxICeokrsLalkP4iYzzpiLVjHHxvVwxSxyRfhfSv-aS40lmo7_I_QkscVcg2TX3njo3DuM1ZGxxPEzKQfONjsyXVvvOqq5X9GtqQS8twvYySu-qBodcV0vfTy7zhhiUThkkag8-kmZkN-iR9vUpWclu6eJL0oYM1FFtF6zZ0fYQWKxi2YbVg_LHqA2UqAh_DJUyeMIEuNG4fAVi2j8FWoYRn-kYCW81Yvxo_SDLVNoxLhOo1WvxzO0_QeU8CcnpKweQNnYZHwCj7tUCj5iQrNBTYWMqGqXFKDcGd0aNayQIgZlB10_KMDkt4Rg-YKt6tgN-Q5R1sPyln3bj8_csnBhC_KIHk5tbeZH4jg7WmXg0tmD0WVuDFYRGrCa-4EhV9iPTHYBU_RWozZp9xGZfAJ_0jTK6ByHrvsOHtE78ChivrCY1Pgto9vrOZcTLFQ%3D%3D)**

clean_body: Angie Craig and Senate Democrats are putting you last. | I am livid. And I am sure it makes you mad, too, Fellow Conservative. The Democrats Shutdown the Government! Chuck Schumer and Senate Democrats voted for illegal immigrants over our veterans. Our active duty military must go without pay. Our veterans won't have the benefits they deserve because of Schumer's shutdown. All because they want free healthcare for illegal immigrants. Are you mad yet? What's worse is that one of my Democrat opponents, Angie Craig, also voted against this same CR to fund the government in the House, even after she called previous government shutdowns "putting politics above people." What is she doing now?! Fellow Conservative, we cannot allow another Schumer lackey to vote against President Trump's America First Agenda - AND YOU. I'm not a career politician. I'm a regular guy from a working-class family who grew up playing hockey and answered our nation's call on my 18th birthday in October 2001, just weeks after the 9/11 attacks. I joined the Marine Corps to defend our country, and as a Navy SEAL, I deployed nine times to over 70 countries, to protect our freedoms and way of life - and it's unthinkable that the men and women who put their lives on the line will go without pay and the benefits they risked their lives to earn. I don't have the funding that my Democrat opponents do - they have the backing of Tim Walz, National Democrats, and the liberal donor base. If you are ready to fight back against career politicians who put politics over people, I humbly ask for your support - That will help keep this grassroots campaign running. Thank you, Adam Schwarze Proud Marine Veteran Retired Navy SEAL Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate – Minnesota FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate Adam Schwarze is a former Navy SEAL and Marine. name: Leslie Parra
subject: United Under the Trees.
body:
https://savetheredwoods.org [https://savetheredwoods.org]
Dear Peter,
During times of division and uncertainty we often seek a place of peace and resilience. For generations, redwood forests have offered a way to come together.
Whether walking beneath their towering canopies or loving them from afar, people find common ground in the redwoods. The trees remind us that resilience is not just about individual strength, but connection. Just as redwoods draw strength from one another underground, our communities thrive when we lift each other up, stand together in the face of adversity, and remain rooted in a shared purpose: redwood conservation and access for all.
https://secure.savetheredwoods.org/a/evergreen-match [https://secure.savetheredwoods.org/a/evergreen-match]
Photo Credit: Vivian Chen
Everyone deserves easy access to the healing power of nature. That’s why I’ve dedicated my work to making experiences with the redwoods more inclusive, ensuring that historically under-resourced communities are welcomed, represented, and empowered. Through programs like Redwood Rides [https://www.savetheredwoods.org/redwoods-magazine/autumn-winter-2024/how-redwood-rides-helps-close-the-nature-gap/] , we’ve helped make that happen by offering free transportation to the parks. Since 2022, we’ve provided 30 rides per year for underrepresented groups to visit the redwoods.
These forests have a unique power to bring people together, and it’s our responsibility to safeguard that for generations to come.
START YOUR MONTHLY GIFT NOW [https://secure.savetheredwoods.org/a/evergreen-match]
Right now, you can maximize your impact with a $250 match when you start an Evergreen Membership. [https://secure.savetheredwoods.org/a/evergreen-match]
Let’s continue to protect these forests not just for their beauty, but for the hope and connection they bring to us all.
Photo of Deborah Zierten [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/STRWL/STRWL/1/89574/images/Leslie-Parra%201.png] With gratitude,
Leslie Parra
Senior Manager of Community Engagement
Save the Redwoods League
Help restore and protect the redwoods

San Francisco, CA 94104
United States

name: Scott Brown
subject: A heartfelt thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scott Brown for New Hampshire, Inc
body:
Fellow Patriot,

I can’t thank you enough. Because of your support, we didn’t just hit our
first end-of-quarter deadline –we crushed it.

When I launched this campaign, I knew we’d be up against entrenched special
interests and an army of liberal donors from across the country. But you
stepped up, and thanks to you, we proved that this team has the grassroots
strength to win.

I’ll never forget the outpouring of support we saw in these first critical
months. Every single donation, large or small, helped us send a powerful
message: our campaign is strong, and we’re ready for the fight ahead.

I hate to ask, but if you’re able to give again, it would mean the world to me
as we kick off this next quarter.
<https://secure.winred.com/scott-brown-for-new-hampshire-inc/september-eoq-thank-you-email-acs/?recurring=true&utm_medium=pemail&utm_source=rs_acs&utm_campaign=20251002_sep-eoq-thank-you_sb&utm_medium=GFY2&utm_source=rcl&utm_campaign=20251002_sep-eoq-thank-you_indirect&utm_term=&utm_content=na>
Starting strong is just as important as finishing strong.
DONATE
<https://secure.winred.com/scott-brown-for-new-hampshire-inc/september-eoq-thank-you-email-acs/?recurring=true&utm_medium=pemail&utm_source=rs_acs&utm_campaign=20251002_sep-eoq-thank-you_sb&utm_medium=GFY2&utm_source=rcl&utm_campaign=20251002_sep-eoq-thank-you_indirect&utm_term=&utm_content=na>
Thank you for standing with me. I’m truly humbled by your trust and support.

Onward,

<https://secure.winred.com/scott-brown-for-new-hampshire-inc/september-eoq-thank-you-email-acs/?recurring=true&utm_medium=pemail&utm_source=rs_acs&utm_campaign=20251002_sep-eoq-thank-you_sb&utm_medium=GFY2&utm_source=rcl&utm_campaign=20251002_sep-eoq-thank-you_indirect&utm_term=&utm_content=na>

name: Kim Schrier
subject: Beyond grateful, but not surprised!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress
body:
I just want to take a moment to say thank you. [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116469242/573521616/693975070?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMzRiZDg0NS1kNzlmLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fuws84GYkOp63oMo3rQ2Wholga-6BpvrBEkxILoUbfs=&emci=053f4a83-a59f-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=134bd845-d79f-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=15820338)

Peter,

I just want to take a moment to say _thank you._ Because of this grassroots team, we ended this quarter strong and were able to hit our September FEC fundraising goal!

This team constantly reminds me that when we come together, we can meet any challenge head-on, **even when things get hard.**

We're constantly fighting an uphill battle against an administration that wants to turn back the clock on healthcare, reproductive freedom, and science. But it's this grassroots team that continues to show up, to speak out, and to stand up for what's right. _That's what gives me hope and keeps me fighting every single day in Congress._

Thank you for all that you do for both me and this campaign,

Kim Rep. Kim Schrier is a doctor, scientist, and mom, fighting for Washington's 8th District in Congress. This is one of the most competitive districts in the country, so we need your help to keep Kim in Congress. clean_body: I just want to take a moment to say thank you. Peter, I just want to take a moment to say thank you. Because of this grassroots team, we ended this quarter strong and were able to hit our September FEC fundraising goal! This team constantly reminds me that when we come together, we can meet any challenge head-on, even when things get hard. We're constantly fighting an uphill battle against an administration that wants to turn back the clock on healthcare, reproductive freedom, and science. But it's this grassroots team that continues to show up, to speak out, and to stand up for what's right. That's what gives me hope and keeps me fighting every single day in Congress. Thank you for all that you do for both me and this campaign, Kim Rep. Kim Schrier is a doctor, scientist, and mom, fighting for Washington's 8th District in Congress. This is one of the most competitive districts in the country, so we need your help to keep Kim in Congress. Chip in now to help protect Kim's seat. | = Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress Contributions or gifts to Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress are not tax deductible. Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress PO Box 2728 Issaquah, WA 98027 United States

[**But we still haven’t heard from YOU. So we’re personally inviting you to THANK Gavin Newsom for defending our democracy! TO: Governor Gavin Newsom
FROM: A grateful Democrat
Subject: Thank you for taking a stand. Dear Governor Newsom,

_I am THRILLED about your redistricting plan to CRUSH the MAGA House majority and end Trump’s presidency as we know it!_

I refuse to sit on the sidelines while Trump attempts to STEAL the 2026 midterms.

**Thank goodness we have you leading the charge to FIGHT BACK!**

I’m doing everything in my power to protect Democrats like you who are brave enough to face Trump head-on and risk MAGA revenge.

[**I know you’re facing relentless attacks from Trump and his MAGA minions, so I’m signing this thank you letter and chipping in $3 to the Voter Protection Project to help Democrats keep fighting for justice and WIN in 2026 »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/94451?t=9&akid=21381%2E878179%2EpYaCxF) | [**Donate $3 to sign now »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/94451?t=10&akid=21381%2E878179%2EpYaCxF)

Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. # MAGA Marine turns the tables on CNN reporter who blames Trump for the gov't shutdown
Marine veteran and GOP Rep. Rich McCormick clashed with CNN's Brianna Keilar when **[she confronted him with a Washington Post poll](https://substack.com/redirect/5fdb06c6-90b0-468c-9477-b12bf4e30d44?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** showing that most Americans blame Trump and the Republicans for the government shutdown.
"Polling is not accurate because the pollsters get out of it wha they want," McCormick quipped back.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/5fdb06c6-90b0-468c-9477-b12bf4e30d44?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

# **[RFK Jr drops AUTISM BOMSHELL on Big Pharma](https://substack.com/redirect/d5b3e6e8-d52f-478a-895a-44677a2db25c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d5b3e6e8-d52f-478a-895a-44677a2db25c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Dear Reader,
RFK Jr just took dead aim on Big Pharma... and he is **NOT** messing around.
In a prime-time press conference he took them to task on everything from Tylenol and autism to COVID vaccines...
And Big Pharma is _**HORRIFIED**_.
For the first time in decades, they're face-to-face with a politician their money _**CAN NOT**_ control. And that means their # 1 fear may be about to come true.
RFK Jr is now poised to EXPOSE **[Big Pharma's biggest secret](https://substack.com/redirect/d5b3e6e8-d52f-478a-895a-44677a2db25c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**.
It's a secret that could cost them hundreds-of-billions in profit and dark money political donations...
Which is why they'll do everything in their power to stop you from hearing **[THIS](https://substack.com/redirect/d5b3e6e8-d52f-478a-895a-44677a2db25c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**. And they may just succeed...
So we're not taking any chances, we're revealing everything TODAY in **[this short video](https://substack.com/redirect/d5b3e6e8-d52f-478a-895a-44677a2db25c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**.
**[Watch it here now](https://substack.com/redirect/d5b3e6e8-d52f-478a-895a-44677a2db25c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** while it's still available.
[Learn More](https://substack.com/redirect/d5b3e6e8-d52f-478a-895a-44677a2db25c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
P.S. It's not every day the US Secretary of Health and Human Services calls out one of Big Pharma's biggest cash cows. But that's exactly what RFK Jr just did. And they're terrified he's going to go one step further by revealing **[Big Pharma's Biggest Secret yet](https://substack.com/redirect/d5b3e6e8-d52f-478a-895a-44677a2db25c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**.
* * *
# Trump declares "armed conflict" with "terrorist" drug cartels
The Trump administration sent a memo to Congress **[declaring the United States is"in a non-international armed conflict"](https://substack.com/redirect/3ee3abdc-4720-4b37-88d9-d4a6aa7a4017?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** with drug cartels, which have been officially designated as terrorist organizations with paramilitary capabilities that directly cause tens of thousands of American deaths annually.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3ee3abdc-4720-4b37-88d9-d4a6aa7a4017?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Military authority justification** : The notification invoked the President's constitutional authority as Commander in Chief to protect Americans and U.S. interests, coming amid Democratic accusations that the administration is exceeding its powers to use military force against cartels, particularly following recent Caribbean strikes.
* **Specific cartel targeting** : The memo cited a September 15 strike against a vessel affiliated with a designated terrorist organization engaged in drug trafficking, part of Trump's broader campaign of military strikes against alleged Venezuelan drug vessels and deployment of Navy guided missile destroyers to the Caribbean.
* **Venezuela tensions escalate** : The administration has specifically targeted Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro, whom they accuse of leading a violent drug cartel and being central to a "narco-terrorism" conspiracy, with the Justice Department placing a $50 million bounty on him while Maduro warns his country is prepared to respond to attacks.

* * *
# Hamas military chief rejects Trump's peace deal
Ezz al-Din al-Hadad, Hamas' top military commander in Gaza who helped plan the October 7, 2023 attack, has **[urged negotiators to reject](https://substack.com/redirect/aa8535f6-a6ae-4a60-bc58-b566929422a9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** President Donald Trump's 20-point cease-fire proposal, calling it a way to eliminate Hamas without achieving any of its goals.
* **Hostages as leverage** : Al-Hadad, known as the "Ghost of al-Qassam," holds significant negotiating power as his forces control the 48 hostages in Gaza (20 believed alive), limiting the influence of Hamas political leaders who showed some openness to the deal with modifications.
* **Key sticking points** : Hamas has rejected the deal's calls for demilitarization unless granted a pathway to Palestinian statehood, and expressed concerns about the proposed transitional government being "another form of occupation," while Palestinian Islamic Jihad also rejected the plan as serving only "Israeli interests."
* **Trump 's deadline and Netanyahu complications**: Trump demanded Hamas provide an official response by Friday or Saturday, warning of "a very sad end" if rejected, though the deal was complicated by Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu's insistence that Israel maintain military presence in parts of Gaza and establish a security zone to "forcibly resist" any Palestinian state.

* * *
# South Carolina AG demands death penalty for cold-blooded killer of 22-year-old woman, prosecutor pushes back
South Carolina Attorney General Alan Wilson is pressuring Fifth Circuit Solicitor Byron Gipson **[to seek the death penalty against Alexander Devonte Dickey, 30,](https://substack.com/redirect/fe32f67f-2d1b-427b-af05-4ce3eaea6d7a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** accused of fatally shooting 22-year-old college student Logan Federico during a May home invasion, citing "clear statutory aggravating factors" and Dickey's extensive criminal history.
The victim's father exploded at lawmakers earlier this week for soft-on-crime policies that enabled his daughter's murder. **[Watch below:](https://substack.com/redirect/206cc4de-9150-48bf-9754-84bfd915844a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
**[Related Video:](https://substack.com/redirect/206cc4de-9150-48bf-9754-84bfd915844a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[](https://substack.com/redirect/206cc4de-9150-48bf-9754-84bfd915844a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Attorney general 's deadline and support**: Wilson gave Gipson until October 10 to declare his intentions and assigned senior attorney Melody Brown to review the case, warning the AG's office would take over prosecution if Gipson was "unable to proceed," with Logan's father Stephen Federico expressing support for Wilson's action.
* **Prosecutor pushback** : Gipson rejected Wilson's deadline as "reckless, irresponsible and unethical," arguing it's too early to make such determinations only four months into the case with key forensic evidence still pending, and noting most of Dickey's 40 prior arrests occurred outside his jurisdiction.
* **Shocking criminal history** : Court documents revealed Dickey had 39 prior arrests and 25 felony charges before Logan's murder, yet had spent barely 600 days in jail over the past decade, with Dickey allegedly shooting Logan in her sleep during a home break-in before going on a shopping spree with her stolen cards.

Key sticking points : Hamas has rejected the deal's calls for demilitarization unless granted a pathway to Palestinian statehood, and expressed concerns about the proposed transitional government being "another form of occupation," while Palestinian Islamic Jihad also rejected the plan as serving only "Israeli interests." Trump 's deadline and Netanyahu complications: Trump demanded Hamas provide an official response by Friday or Saturday, warning of "a very sad end" if rejected, though the deal was complicated by Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu's insistence that Israel maintain military presence in parts of Gaza and establish a security zone to "forcibly resist" any Palestinian state. South Carolina AG demands death penalty for cold-blooded killer of 22-year-old woman, prosecutor pushes back South Carolina Attorney General Alan Wilson is pressuring Fifth Circuit Solicitor Byron Gipson accused of fatally shooting 22-year-old college student Logan Federico during a May home invasion, citing "clear statutory aggravating factors" and Dickey's extensive criminal history. The victim's father exploded at lawmakers earlier this week for soft-on-crime policies that enabled his daughter's murder. Attorney general 's deadline and support: Wilson gave Gipson until October 10 to declare his intentions and assigned senior attorney Melody Brown to review the case, warning the AG's office would take over prosecution if Gipson was "unable to proceed," with Logan's father Stephen Federico expressing support for Wilson's action. Prosecutor pushback : Gipson rejected Wilson's deadline as "reckless, irresponsible and unethical," arguing it's too early to make such determinations only four months into the case with key forensic evidence still pending, and noting most of Dickey's 40 prior arrests occurred outside his jurisdiction. Shocking criminal history : Court documents revealed Dickey had 39 prior arrests and 25 felony charges before Logan's murder, yet had spent barely 600 days in jail over the past decade, with Dickey allegedly shooting Logan in her sleep during a home break-in before going on a shopping spree with her stolen cards. * You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Politibrawl 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

name: George Takei
subject: We cannot afford to lose Jon’s leadership
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jon Ossoff for SenateThis email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail
body:
#

[
---
Hi there, Peter. It’s George Takei. I’m reaching out to you today not as an actor but as a concerned citizen. In a minute, I’m going to ask you to join me in supporting Jon Ossoff’s reelection campaign. If you know the stakes and are ready to pitch in, you can use this link: [**https://act.electjon.com/go/Takei**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-oct25.ZBiurg&refcode=em251002-1-f&t=2&akid=864%2E5691347%2E_1fCN9) Otherwise, let me tell you a little more about Jon Ossoff, and why I am all in to reelect him in 2026: We’ve seen unprecedented levels of corruption and bigotry from this administration. Donald Trump and his advisors are pushing divisive and harmful rhetoric — all while trying to seize more and more power and disintegrate our Constitutionally protected freedoms. I’ve dedicated my adult life to politics and activism because of my family’s experience. When I was five years old, my family and I were put into American internment camps. We lost everything. We were allowed to keep only what we could carry. It was a dark, harrowing time. In the years after we were released from internment, my father instilled in us the vital role we all have in protecting our democracy. He saw what happens to people when elected officials are emboldened to act on their bigoted, xenophobic, and racist beliefs. Seeing the Trump administration’s blatant abuse of power and disregard for human rights, now is the time to stand up and defend our fragile democracy from eroding into authoritarianism. That’s why I’m supporting Jon Ossoff. Before Georgia elected him to serve in the Senate, Jon led a team of investigative journalists that uncovered government corruption and human rights abuses across the world. He is deeply committed to combatting the xenophobia and racism that grips this administration — and knows how to fight back against Trump because it’s not his first time taking on corrupt leaders like him. But Jon is facing what will likely be the most competitive and expensive midterm Senate race in the nation. Republicans are eager to replace him with another rubber stamp for Trump’s agenda. They’ve made him their top target in 2026 and they’re going to spare no expense to flip his seat red. [**We cannot afford to lose his leadership in a time like this. That's why I’m asking if you’ll join me in supporting Jon Ossoff by chipping in whatever you can afford to his reelection campaign today. Any amount helps in a race this critical.**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-oct25.ZBiurg&refcode=em251002-1-f&t=3&akid=864%2E5691347%2E_1fCN9) **[DONATE](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-oct25.ZBiurg&refcode=em251002-1-f&t=4&akid=864%2E5691347%2E_1fCN9)** Live long and prosper, George Takei

name: Michael Bennet
subject: my deepest gratitude
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for Governor
body:
We knocked our Q3 goal out of the park – thank you.
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116470218/573525106/-2054079050?refcode=jtk930-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMTVkMzFkZS1kYjlmLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kSweWLaSzk544K-q6RKCaxpQvpi3MFOgK13YGvXPpfE=)
---
Michael here, folks – before I share the good news, I just wanted to take a moment out of my day to say thank you.
**Thank you** for your commitment to doing everything you can to help me make Colorado the best place to live and raise a family.
**Thank you** for giving your time, money and energy in support of our shared responsibility to build opportunity for one another, and for the next generation.
But _ _ above all, **thank you** for being a part of this extraordinary grassroots team: **B****ecause thanks to the generous contributions made by thousands of folks like you, we hit our $100,000 end-of-quarter target!**
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116470219/573525107/43140476?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk930-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMTVkMzFkZS1kYjlmLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kSweWLaSzk544K-q6RKCaxpQvpi3MFOgK13YGvXPpfE=)
Since I don't rely on corporate megadonors or special interest PACs to run this campaign, this couldn't have happened without everyday folks like you, chipping in $5 here and $10 there to help me prove that there's genuine enthusiasm at the grassroots level to make my vision for Colorado's future a reality.
**While we still have a ways to go until the election, we'll be entering the next chapter of this race with the wind at our backs, and momentum on our side. _[So I hope you know just how much your support means to me, folks, and just how thrilled I am to have you on this team.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116470220/573525108/43140476?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk930-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMTVkMzFkZS1kYjlmLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kSweWLaSzk544K-q6RKCaxpQvpi3MFOgK13YGvXPpfE=)_**
Whether you've been here since April, or just found out about my campaign the other day: From the bottom of my heart, thank you for believing in me.

clean_body: We knocked our Q3 goal out of the park – thank you. | | | = Michael here, folks – before I share the good news, I just wanted to take a moment out of my day to say thank you. Thank you for your commitment to doing everything you can to help me make Colorado the best place to live and raise a family. Thank you for giving your time, money and energy in support of our shared responsibility to build opportunity for one another, and for the next generation. But above all, thank you for being a part of this extraordinary grassroots team: Because thanks to the generous contributions made by thousands of folks like you, we hit our $100,000 end-of-quarter target! = Since I don't rely on corporate megadonors or special interest PACs to run this campaign, this couldn't have happened without everyday folks like you, chipping in $5 here and $10 there to help me prove that there's genuine enthusiasm at the grassroots level to make my vision for Colorado's future a reality. While we still have a ways to go until the election, we'll be entering the next chapter of this race with the wind at our backs, and momentum on our side. = Whether you've been here since April, or just found out about my campaign the other day: From the bottom of my heart, thank you for believing in me. Let's win this thing – together, Michael | | = | Bennet for Governor PO Box 270117 Louisville, CO 80027 United States Paid for by Bennet for Governor. Registered Agent: Tracie Moore. By clicking the links above, you certify that you are responsible for paying all charges incurred in using the debit or credit card to be charged, and that your personal funds will be the true source of the contribution. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Click here to from future emails. |

name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: A game of chicken
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE
body:
[1]Powered By People

Democrats in Congress are holding the line and fighting on the front foot
to reverse the drastic cuts to health care that Republicans rammed through
earlier this year.

I’m glad to see Democrats finally showing some backbone and fighting fire
with fire. Republicans tried to play a game of chicken — but Democrats
haven’t flinched. Trump is trying to force through his extreme agenda
instead of engaging in meaningful compromise, and our leaders have so far
refused to back down.

That matters especially here in Texas where we’re already the
least-insured state in the country.

Families are staring down rising premiums and deductibles while
Republicans refuse to do anything about it. The stakes couldn’t be higher.

This is where Powered by People comes in. We’re mobilizing Texans —
registering new voters, knocking doors, holding town halls to fight back
against Trump's MAGA agenda and elect Democrats up and down the ballot.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251002.WOrkSg&refcode=ema20251002-240388&t=1002&akid=240388%2E444211%2EGcJktH ]If you’re with us, will you chip in today? Your donation keeps
volunteers and organizers on the doors and the phones — exactly where we
need to be.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251002.WOrkSg&refcode=ema20251002-240388&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=240388%2E444211%2EGcJktH ]DONATE $3

Every dollar helps us recruit and train volunteers and expand voter
outreach across Texas.

More soon,

Beto
